title: Multi-pressure rated valve assembly
abstract: A multi-seat bonnet (216) for a valve assembly comprising is disclosed herein. The multi-seat bonnet (216) includes a locking mechanism that is configured to secure the multi-seat bonnet to the valve assembly (202). Further, the multi-seat bonnet (216) includes a first sealing surface (218) configured to engage a high pressure seat (310), in addition to a second sealing surface (220) configured to engage a low pressure seat (412).
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A multi-seat bonnet for a valve assembly comprising: a locking mechanism configured to secure the multi-seat bonnet to the valve assembly; a first sealing surface configured to engage a high pressure seat; and a second sealing surface configured to engage a low pressure seat. 2. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 1, further comprising: a bonnet opening configured to allow a stem of the valve assembly to protrude past the opening such that the stem engages a valve seat. 3. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 2, wherein the first sealing surface is positioned adjacent the bonnet opening. 4. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 3, wherein the first sealing surface comprises: a first surface portion disposed adjacent a first side of the bonnet opening and a second surface portion disposed adjacent a second side of the bonnet opening. 5. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 4, wherein the first surface portion and the second surface portion engage a high pressure seat surface of the high pressure seat to form a high pressure boundary. 6. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 5, wherein the high pressure boundary prevents the high pressure seat from leaking. 7. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 1, wherein the high pressure seat includes a 10,000 PSI pressure rating. 8. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 1, wherein the second sealing surface comprises a first surface portion and a second surface portion that engage, respectively, a first low pressure seat surface and a second low pressure seat surface of the low pressure seat. 9. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 8, wherein the first surface portion and the second surface portion each extend, respectively, at a forty-five-degree angle to the first sealing surface. 10. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 1, wherein the low pressure seat includes a 6,092 PSI pressure rating. 11. The multi-seat bonnet of claim 1, wherein the locking mechanism comprises a bonnet lock. 12. A multi -pressure valve assembly comprising: a valve body; a stem disposed within the valve body, wherein the stem comprises a stem tip configured to form an obstruction with at least one of a high pressure seat and a low pressure seat; a bonnet that encloses the valve body, the bonnet comprising: an opening configured to allow the stem to protrude from the valve body; a first sealing surface configured to form a first seal with a high pressure seat surface; and a second sealing surface configured to form a second seal with a low pressure seat surface. 13. The multi-pressure valve assembly of claim 12, further comprising: a handle coupled to the stem, wherein the handle is actuable to protrude the stem past the bonnet opening. 14. The multi-pressure valve assembly of claim 12, wherein the first sealing surface is configured to form the first seal with a high pressure seat surface that is in accordance with a 10,000 PSI pressure rating. 15. The multi-pressure valve assembly of claim 12, wherein the second sealing surface is configured to form the second seal with a low pressure seat surface that is in accordance with a 6,092 PSI pressure rating. 16. The multi-pressure valve assembly of claim 12, wherein the second sealing surface comprises a first portion and a second portion, the first and second portions being configured to extend at an angle with respect to the first sealing surface. 17. The multi-pressure valve assembly of claim 12, wherein the high pressure seat surface is positioned within a high pressure channel of a high pressure manifold assembly, and the low pressure seat surface is positioned within a low pressure channel of a low pressure manifold assembly, and wherein the first seal forms a pressure boundary that prevents a fluid from escaping the high pressure channel and the second seal forms a pressure boundary that prevents a fluid from escaping the low pressure channel. 18. A method of installing a multi-seat valve assembly with a manifold, comprising: securing a multi-seat bonnet to the multi-seat valve assembly; inserting the multi-seat bonnet into a pressure channel; engaging a sealing surface of the pressure channel with at least one bonnet sealing surface of the multi-seat bonnet; and sealing the multi-seat valve assembly to the manifold. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising: determining a pressure application in accordance with the manifold. 20. The method of claim 18, wherein sealing the multi-seat valve assembly to the manifold comprises: forming a pressure boundary between the at least one bonnet sealing surface and the sealing surface of the pressure channel.
